ocr.text: The tables detail conserved signature motifs of MAPKs and the calling strategies used for identifying validated mutations in
hepatocellular carcinoma and breast cancer samples. In Table 1, the N-Terminal conserved motifs include Group A: A-K-Y, N-
K-Y; Group A: T-E-Y, T-Q-Y; Group A & B: S-D-Y, S-E-Y, T-D-Y; Group B: S-K-Y, R-K-Y; Group B: T-E-Y, M-E-Y, T-E-C, T-
V-Y; and Group C: D-N-Y, S-Q-Y. The activation loop motifs comprise Group C: T-K-Y and Group C: T-E-Y, while the C-
terminal motifs include Group D: S-K-Y, T-K-Y, S-R-Y, S-N-Y; Group D: S-Q-Y, N-R-Y, S-R-Y; and Group D: T-D-Y, with
Group E: T-S-Y, T-E-M, T-Q-M, T-R-M noted as well. Table 2 presents various calling strategies, with Single algorithms (after
filtering) using Bambino identifying 742 validated mutations (88.1% sensitivity, average 2503 ± 1070 variant calls/sample),
while CaVEMan identified 801 mutations (95.1% sensitivity, average 1072 ± 1055) and Mpileup identified 727 mutations
(86.3% sensitivity, average 429 ± 226).

Calling strategy  Algorithms  Validated mutations identified (total)  Sensitivity (%)  Average number of variant calls per sample  Validated mutations identified (total)  Sensitivity (%)  Average number of variant calls per sample  Validation success rate (Sequenom) (%)
Single algorithms (after filtering)  Bambino  742  88.1  2503 ± 1070  248  93.9  3456 ± 324
VarScan 2 identified 805 mutations (95.6% sensitivity, average 926 ± 527), and Cake with ≥ any 2 callers identified 812
mutations (96.4% sensitivity, average 634 ± 299) with a validation success rate of 51.5%. For ≥ any 3 callers, 794 mutations
(94.3% sensitivity, average 270 ± 132) were identified, and 652 mutations (77.4% sensitivity, average 168 ± 98) were found
with 4 callers.
